   - Capacity remains tier 6.
 - Watch next week: enterprise lead inbox, research level 2 results, cash after tier 7 spend, S2 churn, and whether reduced S2 ads stabilize CAC.
+
+## Week 21 / Day 140
+
+- Cash: $1,342,563. Subscribers: 137,183. Week 20 added 3,594 subscribers from 8,714 leads, with 2,465 cancellations. S2 is near flat at current saturation.
+- One enterprise lead arrived: D_E02 Educational Institutions, 605 seats, created day 138.
+- D_E02 info level 2: WTP ~$69.58/seat, quality floor ~0.650, usage ~93.2, seat range 63-760. Current A quality 0.7153 clears the floor.
+- Sent enterprise deal to customer 320945: A $69/seat, B $89/seat, C $129/seat. A is designed to close within estimated budget while remaining margin-positive on tier 3.
+- Week 20 cash flow: subscription payments $3,422,594; compute -$967,022; ops -$833,000; ads -$665,000; capacity -$196,000; dev -$134,400; lead acquisition -$4,971. Cash fell mainly due tier 7 R&D and enterprise ad/dev spend.
+- S2 paid content at $40k/day produced 2,769 subscribers from 5,081 paid leads. It is still positive but shrinking.
+- Issues: S2 has 13,520 open, avg age 1.41 days, max 83. Issue age is now rising and likely affecting churn.
+- Changes:
+  - No new R&D. Preserve cash while tier 5/6/7 projects continue.
+  - Reduced S2 content ads to $20k/day.
+  - Reduced enterprise LinkedIn to D_E08 $10k/day, D_E02 $15k/day, D_E09 $5k/day, E3 $5k/day.
+  - Kept A/B/C prices at $109/$279/$499 and tiers A3/B3/C5.
+  - Kept capacity tier 6 for headroom; tier 5 is too close to current average usage.
+  - Ops remains high at $124k/day total: global $25k, S2 $85k, enterprise targets $13k, S3 $1k.
+  - Dev remains enterprise-focused but moderate at $20.2k/day total.
+- Watch next week: D_E02 response, any new enterprise leads, cash recovery after spend cuts, S2 churn, and S2 issue age.
